ead.archdesc:
======= RMM00039JMerritt family papers39JCompiled by RMC StaffDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
June 20122B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
geneo rghisThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:43:40 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Merritt family papers
Merritt family.
39J
0.6 cubic feet. 0.6 cubic feet.
1841-1893. Correspondence Letters and ledgers from various members of the Merritt family. Collection material in English 7543345
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Ayla Cline
Date completed:
June 2012
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, June 2012
Date modified:
RMC Staff, July 2012
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Letters from various members of the Merritt family, describing life along the Saint Lawrence River and in Saint Lawrence County and their pioneering travel west to Iowa, Wisconsin, Kansas, and Nebraska; much local color. Also several financial ledgers, account and day books, and some various publications.
Cite As:
Merritt family papers, #39J. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Middle West -- Description and travel. Saint Lawrence County (N.Y.) Saint Lawrence River. Merritt family.
7543345 <<<<<<< HEAD Family LettersRecipient: Darwin H. MerrittSeptember 18, 184111
Scope and Contents

Scope and Contents
Address: Liverpool, Onandaga County, NY Sender: Cyrus Burnap Letter #7 Letter seems to be a business transaction. Negative references are made to a Mr. Miller and Mr. Hoffman. Cyrus writes with concern about a money exchange, and the sale of logs.Recipient: Darwin H. MerrittJuly 5, 184611

Sender: Thomas N. Porter Letter #12Recipient: Thomas N. PorterMarch 25, 185012
Scope and Contents
Address: Burlington, VT. Sender: Noadiah Merritt Letter #13 Mr. Merritt addresses a grievance brought up in a previous letter from Mr. Porter. Accusations of property damage are brought up. Mr. Merritt closes by quoting several lines from the Bible: "Do unto others as ye would that they should do unto you." ; "Let him that is without sin cast the first stone." ; "Judge not lest ye be judged."Recipient: William W. MerrittMay 185012

Wilmot, of Pennsylvania, in the House of Representatives discussing slavery in the territoriesMay 3, 185018

Publisher: Congressional Globe OfficeArticle discussing the Northwestern Sanitary Fair (2 pages)June 15, 186518
Scope and Contents
Publisher: Danesville Weekly GazetteEntire page from a newspaper. Various printed advertisements as well as a lengthy article on the report of the New York State Fisheries Commissioners.188418
Scope and Contents
Publisher: UnknownEnvelope advertises Art Metal Light Weight Safes and a promise of burglary insurance18
Scope and Contents
